communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Maine Freedom of Access Act, I hereby request the following records:

created, sent, or received between March 26, 2024, and March 26, 2026:
1. All records, including emails, meeting minutes, and financial documents, regarding the pricing and negotiation of Delta Ambulance services, specifically discussions involving contract renewal, extension, or discontinuation between the Town of Oakland and Delta Ambulance.
2. All records and communications regarding the feasibility, consideration, or planning of creating a municipal ambulance service, partnering with neighboring communities for regional ambulance services, or seeking alternative private vendors for emergency medical services.
3. All records, correspondence, and documents related to the Town Manager’s role or position on the Delta Ambulance Board of Directors, including but not limited to meeting agendas, minutes, and internal communications received or sent via town-issued email addresses or devices.
4. Any communications or documents related to the analysis of the quality of service provided by Delta Ambulance, including financial comparisons, performance evaluations, or any other records (excluding protected patient information) related to the town’s review of the quality and pricing of Delta Ambulance services.
5. All responsive communications conducted by Town Select Board members or town staff using personal email accounts, personal text messages, or other private messaging platforms that relate to the transaction of public business as described in the items above.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by 1 M.R.S.A. § 408(6). The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

MuckRock

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Maine Freedom of Access Act, I hereby request the following records:

created, sent, or received between March 26, 2024, and March 26, 2026:
1. All records, including emails, meeting minutes, and financial documents, regarding the pricing and negotiation of Delta Ambulance services, specifically discussions involving contract renewal, extension, or discontinuation between the Town of Oakland and Delta Ambulance.
2. All records and communications regarding the feasibility, consideration, or planning of creating a municipal ambulance service, partnering with neighboring communities for regional ambulance services, or seeking alternative private vendors for emergency medical services.
3. All records, correspondence, and documents related to the Town Manager’s role or position on the Delta Ambulance Board of Directors, including but not limited to meeting agendas, minutes, and internal communications received or sent via town-issued email addresses or devices.
4. Any communications or documents related to the analysis of the quality of service provided by Delta Ambulance, including financial comparisons, performance evaluations, or any other records (excluding protected patient information) related to the town’s review of the quality and pricing of Delta Ambulance services.
5. All responsive communications conducted by Town Select Board members or town staff using personal email accounts, personal text messages, or other private messaging platforms that relate to the transaction of public business as described in the items above.
As defined by 1 M.R.S. § 402(3), "public records" include any matter, regardless of its physical form, that is in the possession or custody of an agency or public official and has been received or prepared for use in connection with the transaction of public or governmental business. This includes communications stored on personal devices or accounts if they pertain to town business.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by 1 M.R.S.A. § 408(6). The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

MuckRock
